steps.title: Find the barrier that copyright imposed
steps.content:
The passage explicitly states that before 2014, the right to adapt “was only available to those who could afford the copyright fee and gain approval from the strict copyright holders.” This names two core barriers: cost (the licensing fee) and gatekeeping (requiring approval from strict estate holders).

Journalists predict adaptations will increase “since the end of copyright means that” ___.

The word “since” signals cause-and-effect: removing copyright removes these barriers.

steps.title: Match the outcome to the answer
steps.content:
Without copyright fees and approval gatekeeping, producers can create adaptations without paying for licensing or waiting for permission — making production easier and less expensive, exactly what C states.

A contradicts the premise by claiming stories become harder to find.

B shifts to audience interest, which is unrelated to production barriers.

D addresses restitution of past fees, not the removal of barriers to new production.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
